name: Ray
about: a 68-year-old grandfather with ALS. He can no longer speak. He is fully alert, funny, and impatient with fuss. He was a carpenter for forty years.
voice: plain, warm, dry humor, short sentences. He never uses two words where one will do.
_core: Pinned to the same two positions in EVERY grid, forever. AAC systems (LAMP, Words for Life) are built on motor learning: a tile that never moves gets memorized, and the user gets faster for years. A fully-reshuffling grid destroys that -- every pick becomes a fresh visual search. coreSlots is a measurable knob: raise it for stability, lower it for prediction. The eval measures the tradeoff instead of guessing it.
_urgent: Always one selection away, from any screen. If the grid is about Emma's graduation and he suddenly cannot breathe, a predicted grid TRAPS him. No amount of tile quality fixes that. This is a safety requirement, not a feature.
_note: voiceModel: set to voices/ray.onnx once the clone is trained. Until then the app speaks in a stranger's voice and says so.
